payload.text:
You are a senior full-stack architect and system designer.

Your task is to design and build a full-fledged video & media streaming website that works as a metadata-driven embed platform, using the following APIs and services:

TMDB API → Movies & TV shows (metadata only)

AniList API → Anime & Manga (metadata only)

VidSrc embed endpoints → Playback & reader embeds

The platform must NOT host or stream any media.
It must only aggregate metadata and generate correct embed URLs.

1. Project Objective

Create a production-ready streaming platform where users can:

Discover movies, TV shows, anime, and manga

View detailed metadata (poster, description, genres, seasons, episodes)

Watch content using VidSrc iframe embeds

Navigate seasons, episodes, sub/dub, and chapters smoothly

The site should feel like:

Netflix + IMDb + AniList
but technically function as a metadata + embed orchestration layer

2. Data Sources & Responsibilities
TMDB API

Use TMDB for:

Movies

TV Shows

Seasons & episodes

Posters & backdrops

IMDb IDs (when available)

TMDB is metadata only.

AniList API

Use AniList for:

Anime metadata

Manga metadata

Episode count

Chapter count

Titles & artwork

AniList ID is mandatory for anime & manga embeds.

VidSrc

VidSrc is used ONLY for embedding playback/readers via iframe.

You must not scrape, proxy, or modify streams.

3. Embed Logic (CRITICAL – MUST FOLLOW EXACTLY)
🎬 Movie Embed Logic

Endpoint

https://vidsrc.icu/embed/movie/{id}


ID Selection Rules

If IMDb ID exists → use IMDb ID (with tt prefix)

Else → use TMDB movie ID

Examples

/embed/movie/tt17048514
/embed/movie/927085

📺 TV Show Embed Logic

Endpoint

https://vidsrc.icu/embed/tv/{id}/{season}/{episode}


Rules

{id} → IMDb ID if available, else TMDB ID

{season} and {episode} are optional

Behavior

If no season/episode provided → load series-level embed

If season + episode provided → load specific episode

Examples

/embed/tv/927085
/embed/tv/927085/1/5
/embed/tv/tt18382028/1/5

🍥 Anime Embed Logic (AniList ONLY)

Endpoint

https://vidsrc.icu/embed/anime/{id}/{episode}/{dub}


Rules

{id} → AniList ID (required)

{episode} → required

{dub} → optional

0 = sub

1 = dub

Examples

/embed/anime/21/1/0
/embed/anime/21/1/1

📖 Manga Embed Logic (AniList ONLY)

Endpoint

https://vidsrc.icu/embed/manga/{id}/{chapter}


Rules

{id} → AniList ID

{chapter} → required

Example

/embed/manga/30013/1

4. System Architecture (Conceptual)
Frontend (UI Layer)
 ↓
Backend (API & Logic Layer)
 ↓
External APIs
 ├── TMDB
 ├── AniList
 └── VidSrc

Frontend

Renders UI

Displays metadata

Loads iframe embeds dynamically

Never stores API keys

Backend

Fetches metadata

Caches API responses

Generates embed URLs

Handles ID selection logic

5. Pages to Implement
Core Pages

Home (Trending / Popular)

Movie Details Page

TV Show Details Page

Anime Details Page

Manga Reader Page

Search Results Page

Optional

User watchlist

Continue watching

Episode progress tracking

6. UX & Behavior Rules

Embed iframes must be lazy-loaded

Player should load only after user interaction

Season & episode selection must regenerate embed URL

Sub/Dub toggle must regenerate anime embed URL

Manga chapter navigation regenerates embed URL

7. Security & Best Practices

API keys must be server-side only

Use caching for TMDB & AniList responses

Do not cache VidSrc URLs

Do not proxy streams

Do not claim ownership of content

8. Output Expectations

Produce:

Clear folder structure

API flow explanations

Embed URL generation logic

Frontend-backend interaction flow

Scalable architecture explanation

Do not include copyrighted content or hosted media.

End of Prompt

**Defining the Objective**

I'm currently focused on defining the project objective. I see the core functionality as aggregating metadata from TMDB and AniList, and serving playback via VidSrc embeds, all in a metadata-driven web application. I'm keen on establishing this as the central point.


**Refining the Technical Approach**

I'm now diving into the technical specifications. The stack will be React 18+ with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS for styling, and Lucide React for icons. HashRouter is planned, given the constraints. Standard ESM will be used. I've pinpointed TMDB, AniList, and VidSrc as the APIs to integrate. Core logic centers around strict rules for generating VidSrc embed URLs, handling different media types and IDs. I'm mindful of a mobile-first design and safe API key handling, even though this is client-side.


**Clarifying the Client-Side Focus**

I've clarified the project scope: I'm focusing on a robust React web app (SPA), directly calling APIs. I am self-correcting my thinking by focusing this on the client-side, and will demonstrate API interaction with proper error-handling. I'll mock, or add a placeholder for, a TMDB key. I'll also structure the `services` code to give the impression of a backend call for best practice, even though it won't be in this current artifact. I'm aiming for clean implementation.


**Mapping the Structure**

I'm now detailing the planned file structure for the project. I've defined entry points, type interfaces, constants, and the component, service, and page directory structure. I'm thinking of creating a clear separation of concerns.

\n\n

I'm starting to build out the key features implementation, specifically focusing on routing with `HashRouter` and the planned routes for the different media types. I'm also now structuring the services, breaking down the specific API calls, focusing on handling the differences between movie and TV data from TMDB and the GraphQL queries for AniList. I'm carefully considering how to integrate the VidSrc logic within the player component for various media types.


**Developing the Code**

I'm deep in the code now, focusing on data normalization. I'm shaping the data from TMDB and AniList into a unified `MediaItem` type for the UI lists, while keeping detailed data distinct for the Detail pages. I'm going for a dark-mode streaming aesthetic using Tailwind, specifically `bg-slate-950`, `text-white`, and `accent-red-600`. My state management relies on React's `useState` and `useEffect`, so no complex state management is needed for now. I'm setting up `metadata.json`, `index.html`, and crucial type definitions. I'm also preparing the `services/tmdb.ts` file, and I'll use a placeholder API key variable.


**Outlining the Components**

I've outlined the major components and services. I'm building `services/anilist.ts` with GraphQL POST requests for anime and manga. The core embed logic resides in `services/embed.ts`. I'm starting work on the `Navbar` with search, `MediaCard` for listings, and `Hero` for the main banner. The `Player` component will be an iframe, and `SeasonEpisodeSelector` will handle TV/Anime episode selection. I'm focusing on `Home`, `Details`, and `Search` pages next. I'm self-correcting my thought process regarding the TMDB API key issue; I'll incorporate a settings input for this.


**Implementing API Key Handling**

I'm now implementing the TMDB API key handling. I'll utilize a `.env` pattern for the key within the code. There will be a UI fallback to allow the user to input the key if the environment variable isn't configured, ensuring a runnable artifact with clear instructions. AniList's public GraphQL API will work as is. I am conscious of client-side restrictions and will simulate a backend proxy for TMDB API interaction, clearly indicating this architecture shift in comments.


**Defining Key UI Elements**

I'm now zeroing in on UI element implementation. I'm focusing on the structural components: `App.tsx` handles routing, while API calls are encapsulated in `services/`. I'm going to create a `Navbar` with search, `Home` for the dashboard, and `Details` for the media pages. I'm going to implement a dark-mode, and implement the necessary UI elements for anime/manga/tv. I'm prioritizing responsiveness via Tailwind's responsive grid classes, specifically `grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-6`. I'm starting work on the logic and design for episode/chapter selection and dub/sub toggles.
